steps.title: Read Holme's reaction
steps.content: Holme does not put down his book when the letter arrives. He glances indifferently at the letter and impatiently at the postman, and the narrator says he regards interruptions while reading Gibbon as insults.

steps.title: Infer his feeling
steps.content: Choice A invents relief, Choice B invents excitement about a friend, and Choice C invents sadness about the postman not talking. The evidence points to irritation because the delivery interrupted his reading. The answer is D.
